heroHeadline: Sheinbaum at 569 Days: Tren Felipe Ángeles Inaugurated to AIFA; CIA Agents Confirmed Unauthorized; Sheinbaum Solidarity Message After WHC Dinner Shooting
heroSubtitle: On April 26 — a Sunday — President Claudia Sheinbaum co-presided with Estado de México Governor Delfina Gómez at the inauguration of the Tren Felipe Ángeles, the 23.7-km suburban express linking Buenavista station to AIFA airport in 43 minutes. The train — nationalized April 23 for 5,999 million pesos (buyout of Spanish firms CAF and Omnitren) — opens with fares from MXN $11.50 to $45 and serves 82,000 residents in four Estado de México municipalities, completing a key FIFA World Cup 2026 infrastructure milestone before the June 11 tournament kick-off. In a parallel development, Mexico's Security Cabinet formally confirmed that both CIA agents who died in the April 20 Chihuahua Sierra Tarahumara crash had entered Mexico without operational authorization — one on a tourist visa, one on a diplomatic passport — intensifying the sovereignty dispute that has put Chihuahua Governor Maru Campos in federal crosshairs; Sheinbaum announced she will present a full evidence report at her Monday April 27 mañanera, while the Senate scheduled hearings for April 28. Separately, following shots fired near the White House Correspondents' Dinner entrance on April 25, Sheinbaum posted a diplomatic message of solidarity: 'Violence should never be the way.' The peso recovered marginally to approximately 17.41 MXN/USD on April 26 from 17.42 April 24, supported by low weekend trading volumes.
footerNote: Data sourced from Diario Oficial, INEGI, Banco de México, SNSP, Secretaría de Economía, INAH, Reuters, AP, El Universal, Reforma, Animal Político, Mexico News Daily, Infobae, Milenio, La Jornada, Al Jazeera, Bloomberg, AtlasIntel, USTR, Chatham House, Mexico Business News, El Financiero, La Silla Rota, Proceso, CNN Español, Lopez Doriga, The Yucatan Times, Excelsior, La Razón de México, Quinto Poder, PBS NewsHour, NBC News, Time, NPR, CBC, Japan Kantei, Japan MOFA, SDP Noticias, Eje Central, TVAzteca, UnoTV, CBS News, El Mañana de Nuevo Laredo, Bloomberg Línea, Canadian PMO, UPI, MLB.com, El Imparcial, Expansión Política, Heraldo Binario, FONADIN
